summary: Make the rotated-provider-secret persistence test submit its form directly and prove the request stream is invoked exactly once without expanded timeouts or retries.

externalInputs.purpose: Observed the exact final-main aggregate failure and its retained DOM showing an enabled Send control, unchanged textarea, empty message list, and no submitted request.

decisions.decision: Use one direct form submit with a stable named request-stream spy, assert exactly one request synchronously, and restore default wait and test timeouts.
decisions.rationale: The aggregate DOM proved the synthetic button click never submitted, and increasing its wait had already failed twice. This test concerns secret reload/redaction rather than button wiring, which other tests cover. Direct form submission plus an exact call count distinguishes submission failure from streaming or persistence failure without a retry or larger budget.

testEvidence.criterion: A rotated provider secret is used for one submitted request and is redacted from every durable conversation snapshot.
testEvidence.test: Submit the owned form once, require the request stream to be called once, observe the replacement-secret echo, and require retained snapshots to contain only the redaction marker.

red.command: GitHub Actions Web checks, run 29977722439, job 89112948223
red.outcome: RED: 1 of 1,741 executed tests failed after 10 seconds; the retained DOM showed the enabled Send control and unchanged input but no conversation messages, proving no request was submitted.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/ai/AiConversationPanel.test.tsx -t "reloads a rotated provider key before every persistence transaction" --reporter=default
green.outcome: GREEN: the exact focused test passed in 74 ms with one form submission, one request-stream invocation, default timeouts, and no retry.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
